2604.20523.title: Early-Stage Product Line Validation Using LLMs: A Study on Semi-Formal Blueprint Analysis
2604.20523.paper_summary: We study whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can perform feature model analysis operations (AOs) directly on semi-formal textual blueprints, i.e., concise constrained-language descriptions of feature hierarchies and constraints, enabling early validation in Software Product Line scoping. Using 12 state-of-the-art LLMs and 16 standard AOs, we compare their outputs against the solver-based oracle FLAMA. Results show that reasoning-optimized models (e.g., Grok 4 Fast Reasoning, Gemini 2.5 Pro) achieve 88-89% average accuracy across all evaluated blueprints and operations, approaching solver correctness. We identify systematic errors in structural parsing and constraint reasoning, and highlight accuracy-cost trade-offs that inform model selection. These findings position LLMs as lightweight assistants for early variability validation.
2604.20523.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究大型語言模型（LLMs）是否能夠直接在半正式文本藍圖上執行特徵模型分析操作（AOs），即對特徵層級和約束的簡明限制語言描述，從而實現軟體產品線範疇的早期驗證。<lb/>使用12個最先進的LLMs和16個標準AOs，我們將它們的輸出與基於求解器的預言者FLAMA進行比較。<lb/>結果顯示，經過推理優化的模型（例如，Grok 4 Fast Reasoning，Gemini 2.5 Pro）在所有評估的藍圖和操作中達到了88-89%的平均準確率，接近求解器的正確性。<lb/>我們識別了結構解析和約束推理中的系統性錯誤，並突出了影響模型選擇的準確性與成本之間的權衡。<lb/>這些發現使LLMs成為早期變異驗證的輕量級助手。
2604.20523.author: Viet-Man Le et.al.
2604.20523.authors: Viet-Man Le, Thi Ngoc Trang Tran, Sebastian Lubos, Alexander Felfernig, Damian Garber
